---
applyTo: "**/*.tf,**/*.tfvars"
---

## Terraform Rules for This Repository

### Provider & Versions
- Provider: AWS `~> 6.0` — always check changelog before upgrading
- Terraform version: `>= 1.5` (CI pins `~> 1.9`)
- Always run `terraform fmt` before committing
- Validate with: `terraform init -backend=false && terraform validate && terraform fmt -check`

### Module Structure
- `variables.tf` (inputs), `outputs.tf` (outputs), `versions.tf` (provider constraints), `main.tf` (resources)
- Feature flags use `count = var.enable_<feature> ? 1 : 0` — never `for_each` for boolean toggles
- Access feature-flagged module outputs as `module.<name>[0].<output>`
- Never write `.tfvars` files by hand — they are generated by Python scripts via `hcl_writer.py`

### State Management
- S3 backend: bucket `acme-lz-terraform-state`, lock table `acme-lz-terraform-locks`
- State key for account-setup: `account-setup/{account_id}/terraform.tfstate`
- Each stack manages its own state file — never cross-reference state between stacks directly

### AWS Resource Conventions
- Every resource must include tags: `Org`, `Program`, `Owner`, `CostCenter`, `ManagedBy = "terraform"`
- S3 buckets must always have: `aws_s3_bucket_versioning`, `aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration` (KMS), `aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block`
- KMS keys must enable automatic key rotation
- IAM roles follow least-privilege — never use `"Action": "*"` or `"Resource": "*"` in policies
- EBS encryption must be enabled by default: `aws_ebs_encryption_by_default`
- CloudTrail must be organization-wide with `is_organization_trail = true`

### Multi-Account Patterns
- Use `aws.target` provider alias for cross-account resource deployment into member accounts
- Use `aws.sso` provider alias for Identity Center (SSO) operations
- Default `aws` provider targets the management account
- Permission sets reference customer-managed policies by NAME (not ARN) — policies exist in target accounts
- Use `aws_ssoadmin_customer_managed_policy_attachment` — never `aws_ssoadmin_managed_policy_attachment` for custom policies
- CloudFormation StackSets use `SERVICE_MANAGED` deployment mode for org-wide rollout
- Use `aws_organizations_organization` as data source (not resource) when reading org state across stacks

### Organizations & Governance
- SCPs deny operations outside allowed regions (`us-east-1`, `us-west-2`) — must exempt global services (IAM, STS, Organizations, CloudFront, Route53, S3)
- Security guardrail SCP prevents disabling: GuardDuty, Security Hub, Config, CloudTrail, Access Analyzer
- Root denial SCP blocks all root user actions in member accounts
- OU structure: Security → Infrastructure → Workloads (Prod/Staging/Dev) → Sandbox

### Security Services (always-on in landing zone)
- GuardDuty: org-wide, delegated admin to security account
- Security Hub: centralized findings, standards subscriptions (CIS, AWS Best Practices)
- AWS Config: conformance packs, org aggregator, compliance tracking
- CloudTrail: organization trail, multi-region, log file validation enabled
- Access Analyzer: cross-account access auditing in every member account
- Inspector: vulnerability scanning, delegated admin
